findings.detail: Reads cleanly as the three calibration states arranged on a vertical axis that echoes the reliability-diagram geometry: Above the diagonal (underconfident) at top, On the diagonal (well-calibrated, green) as the central reference, Below the diagonal (overconfident, red) at bottom. Edge labels 'more accurate than it claims' / 'less accurate than it claims' make the deviation direction explicit. Diagonal is the visual reference; below = dangerous overconfident case is carried by both color (red, thick border) and text. Axis labels (x = stated confidence, y = observed accuracy) stated in the title banner.

cycle_history.detail: First version laid out 3 independent column-pairs (diagonal | above | below) so the diagonal did not read as the central REFERENCE the other two states deviate from. Geometry of the reliability diagram (above = higher accuracy, below = lower accuracy) was not echoed. Revised to a single vertical path with the diagonal at center.

stats.layout: flowchart TB (single vertical path: above -> diagonal -> below)
stats.color_tiers: green = on diagonal (well-calibrated, trustworthy); red = below (overconfident, dangerous); blue = above (underconfident)
stats.scope_creep_check: no ECE / temperature-scaling / multi-class / confusion-matrix nodes present
